title: rubus occidentalis var. exsuccus
intro: Rubus occidentalis var. exsuccus, also known as the western thimbleberry, is a rare and unique variety of the western thimbleberry plant, a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Rubus occidentalis var. exsuccus include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its ability to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
